docs.title: Clover Training
docs.text:
Download Full Training (PDF)

Select a section from the sidebar to get started.

Section Description General POS login, menu selection Shifts Clock in, clock out, shift report, tip out Opening Orders Starting a new order Updating Orders Modifying an open order Closing Orders Bills and payment

docs.title: Record Cash Events
docs.text:
Cash events record the reason for putting cash in or taking cash out of the register. Common use: server tip payouts.

  1. Open Clover Dining
  2. Tap the three dots in the top right → tap Open Cash Drawer
  3. Select Add or Remove
  4. Enter the amount and reason → tap Continue (right side)
  5. The drawer opens automatically — add or remove the cash as recorded

Note

Cash events are recorded on the Bartender → Terminal Settlement Report.

docs.title: Terminal Settlement Report
docs.text:
The Terminal Settlement Report shows all transactions for the bartender's shift, including all cash events.

  1. Launch the TSR app from the Clover home screen
  2. The app auto-detects and populates the current shift for the bartender PIN logged into the device
  3. If the shift dates are incorrect, manually update them
  4. Tap Generate Report — the report displays in the right panel
  5. Tap Print Report to print

docs.title: Bill Printing
docs.text:
There are two options for printing bills: Print Entire Bill or Print Individual Bills.

  • Print Entire Bill prints the full order on one bill.
  • Print Individual Bills prints a separate bill per guest. Items assigned to whole table are split equally across each guest's bill.

Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from another workflow: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find it on the Tables tab or Bar Tab and tap to open.

docs.title: On Station
docs.text: From the order, tap one of the two buttons at the bottom — Print Entire Bill or Print Individual Bills.

docs.title: On Flex
docs.text:
1. Tap the green pay button (top left) → tap three dots (bottom right)
  2. Choose Print Entire Bill or Print Individual Bills

Note

Regardless of which print format is chosen, payment can still accommodate any combination of guests. See Closing Orders → Take Payment.

docs.title: Take Payment
docs.text:
Payment can be split across several methods. The split method is independent of the format used to print the bill — see Closing Orders → Bill Printing.

Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from another workflow: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find it on the Tables tab or Bar Tab and tap to open.

docs.title: On Station
docs.text: Tap the green Pay button under the order summary on the left. The payment screen opens with the default of one payment for the full order. Alternative payment options appear as tabs across the top. Select the desired tab to take each payment.

docs.title: On Flex
docs.text:
1. Tap the green pay box (top left) → tap the green Pay button (bottom right)
  2. The payment screen opens with the default of one payment for the full order. To switch modes, tap the three dots (top right).

docs.title: Payment options
docs.text:
• Split Custom Amount — can be used to split the full order amount evenly among all guests, or any other split the guests want.
  • Split By Items — select specific items to include in this payment.
  • Split By Guest — pay each guest's individual bill. A single guest can also cover the bill for a subset of guests.

docs.title: Pay Full Amount
docs.text: The default payment screen accepts payment for the full amount of the order. Swipe the credit card or enter the amount of cash paid to complete the transaction. If credit is used, the receipt prints automatically. If cash is used, the POS prompts the server to optionally print the receipt.

docs.title: Split Payment
docs.text:
If the order is paid across more than one payment:

  1. After each payment, the receipt prints automatically if credit was used. If cash was used, the POS prompts the server to optionally print the receipt. The remaining order total then appears.
  2. Tap Next Payment to take the next partial payment
  3. The order remains open and the table remains seated until the full order total has been covered

docs.title: Tip Adjustment
docs.text:
Record tips that customers handwrite on their printed credit-card receipts.

Prerequisite

The order must be closed with at least one credit payment, and the guest must have written the tip on the printed receipt.

  1. Note the Transaction number on the printed receipt where the guest wrote the tip
  2. From the home screen, launch the Tips app
  3. Find the row where Trans # matches the transaction number on the receipt
  4. Tap the row — a keypad opens for the tip amount
  5. Enter the tip — the Total auto-calculates from Subtotal + Tip
  6. Tap Done (top right on Flex, bottom left on Station)

The tip is recorded. The Tips app stays open to enter more tips.

docs.title: Menu Selection
docs.text:
Two menus are available:

  • Default POS Menu
  • Banquet Menu

To switch between them:

  1. Open any order within Clover Dining
  2. Tap the three dots in the top right
  3. Select the desired menu → tap Save

The selected menu stays configured on that specific device.

docs.title: POS Login
docs.text:
Clover requires 4-digit PINs. The old POS used 3-digit PINs.

Your existing PIN still works

Add a leading zero to make it 4 digits.

Example: 253 becomes 0253.

docs.title: Unlock the device
docs.text: Enter the PIN on the screen.

docs.title: Lock the device or switch accounts
docs.text: Tap the lock button (bottom right) → enter the next PIN.

docs.title: Open Member Order
docs.text:
1. Open the MemberLink app from the home screen
  2. Scan the member ID card, or type the member's name or number and tap Search
  3. Tap the member from the search results
  4. Verify membership status is active
  5. Tap New Order — Clover Dining opens and the new order appears at the top of the Bar Tab with the member's name
  6. Tap the order to open it
  7. Enter the guest count and tap Save

  8. If serving at a table, move the order to the seated table. Steps vary by device:

    • On Station: tap Move Order to An Available Table → tap the table number. The table turns blue once seated. Tap the blue table again to open the order.
    • On Flex: tap the green dollar square (top left) → three dots (bottom right) → Move Order → tap the table number → tap the blue row to open the order.

    If serving at the bar, skip this step — the order stays on the Bar Tab.

  9. Proceed with taking the order — go to Updating Orders → Add & Remove Items

Don't leave the order empty

Add at least one item before navigating away. Clover Dining auto-deletes orders that have no items on them.

docs.title: Open Non-Member Order
docs.text:
1. Open the Clover Dining app from the home screen
  2. Navigate to the Tables tab or the Bar Tab depending on where the customer is seated

  3. Open the order:

    • If serving at a table: tap the corresponding table number for the seated table.
    • If serving at the bar: tap the plus button (bottom right) to open a new bar tab.

  4. Enter the guest count and tap Save

  5. Proceed with taking the order — go to Updating Orders → Add & Remove Items

Don't leave the order empty

Add at least one item before navigating away. Clover Dining auto-deletes orders that have no items on them.

docs.title: Clock In
docs.text:
1. Open the Shifts app from the home screen
  2. Tap Clock in (bottom right)
  3. Check the box if holding cash sales this shift
  4. Tap Clock In

docs.title: Clock Out
docs.text:
1. Open the Shifts app from the home screen
  2. Tap Clock Out (bottom right)
  3. Tap Clock Out

docs.title: Server Shift Report
docs.text:
The shift report shows whether the house owes the server tip settlements or the server owes the house cash back. Print it after every shift and include it in the cash bag.

  1. Open the Shifts app from the home screen
  2. Tap the row for the shift just clocked out of
  3. Verify the Holding cash sales box is correct — fix it if wrong
  4. Tap Print report
  5. Include the printout in the cash bag

docs.title: Tip Out Calculator
docs.text:
The Tip Out Calculator provides a suggested tip-out breakdown to kitchen and bar staff at the end of a shift.

Clock out first

Clock out of the shift via the Shifts app before opening the Tip Out Calculator.

  1. Tap the Tip Out Calculator from the home screen
  2. The app loads the latest shift and displays the suggested tip-out breakdown

docs.title: Add & Remove Items
docs.text:
Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from Opening Orders: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find it on the Tables tab or Bar Tab and tap to open.

Reminder

Ensure the guest count is correct. If needed, see Updating Orders → Modify Guest Count.

docs.title: Add an item
docs.text:
For multi-guest orders, ensure the correct guest is selected prior to adding items. Tap whole table for shared items, or tap the specific guest. On Station, the guest list is in the order summary on the left. On Flex, guests appear as icons to the right of the green dollar box — scroll horizontally to see more.

Next, select the category for the item from the categories bar:

  • On Station: second column from the left, to the right of the order summary.
  • On Flex: third row from the top — slide horizontally to see more categories.

Items in the selected category appear below (on Flex) or to the right (on Station). Tap an item name once to add it to the order. Each tap adds one count of the item.

If the item requires modifier choices, the modifier options display after the item is tapped.

docs.title: Custom Modifiers
docs.text:
1. Tap the item from the order summary
  2. Tap the Custom Modifier box to open the keyboard

There is only one custom modifier entry field. To add multiple custom modifiers, use the newline button on the keyboard to separate them.

docs.title: Remove an item
docs.text:
Steps vary by device:

  • On Station: find the item on the left order panel → tap it once → tap Delete.
  • On Flex: tap the green dollar box (top left) to open the order → find the item → tap it → tap Delete.

docs.title: Combining Tables
docs.text:
Two tables can be combined by merging one table into another.

Prerequisite

The orders for each of the two tables must already be created and active.

Note

The first order will be merged into the second order.

Within Clover Dining, navigate to the first order and open it.

docs.title: On Station
docs.text:
1. Tap three dots from the bottom menu → tap Combine Orders
  2. Navigate to the Bar or Tables tab where the destination order resides
  3. Tap the order or table — the first order combines into the second

To verify, tap the destination order. The order summary on the left shows the moved guests with a notation in parentheses indicating which table they came from.

docs.title: On Flex
docs.text:
1. Tap three dots in the bottom right of the first order → tap Combine Orders
  2. Tap the bar or table order to merge into
  3. Tap Combine Orders to complete the merge

docs.title: Coursing & Firing
docs.text: Inventory items are preconfigured to fire to the correct printer. Firing an order sends each item to its assigned printer.

docs.title: Fire All
docs.text:
Add all items for the current round first (for example, the first round of drinks), then fire the order.

  • On Station: the Fire All button appears in the bottom left under the order summary.
  • On Flex: tap the green pay box → tap Fire All (bottom left).

docs.title: Manual Coursing
docs.text: After the first round is fired, return later to add the next round (for example, dinner after drinks). Tap Fire All again — only the newly added items fire.

docs.title: Refire or reprint an item
docs.text:
A manual refire can be triggered at any time for any item — useful when an item needs to be reprinted or modified.

  • On Station: open the order → tap three dots in the bottom menu → tap Refire to Kitchen.
  • On Flex: open the order → tap the green pay box → tap three dots (bottom right) → tap Refire to Kitchen.

The refire screen pre-checks only items that have not been fired yet. To refire an already-fired item, check its box manually, then tap Print (bottom right).

docs.title: Modify Guest Count
docs.text:
Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from another workflow: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find it on the Tables tab or Bar Tab and tap to open.

docs.title: On Station
docs.text: The order summary on the left displays the guest count in blue at the top. Tap the blue text → change the number of guests → tap Save.

docs.title: On Flex
docs.text: The guest bar is to the right of the green dollar box. Scroll to the far right of the guest bar — the guest count appears in blue. Tap the blue count → enter the new guest count → tap Save.

docs.title: Moving Guests
docs.text:
Guests can be moved to a new table or join an already seated table.

Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from another workflow: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find the order for the table or bar area where the guest is currently seated. Tap to open.

docs.title: On Station
docs.text:
1. In the order summary on the left, find the guest's row → tap three dots → tap Move Guest
  2. Select the Bar or Tables tab depending on the destination
  3. Select the bar or table order to move the guest to

After the move, the previous order displays without that guest. To see the moved guest on the new order, tap the back button in the bottom navigation menu (back arrow), then select the Bar or Tables area and tap the destination table. The moved guest appears on the order summary with a notation in parentheses showing which table they came from.

docs.title: On Flex
docs.text:
1. Tap the green pay button to open the order summary
  2. Find the guest → tap three dots on their guest row → tap Move Guest
  3. Select the destination Bar or Tables area
  4. Select the table number to move the guest to

After the move, the previous order opens without that guest.

Reminder

If the guest was a member and assigned to the old order, the membership assignment will need to be manually moved to the new order. Unassign the member from the previous order and reassign to the new order. See Updating Orders → Reassign Member.

docs.title: Moving Items
docs.text:
Items may need to be moved from one guest to another, or from the whole table to one guest.

Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from another workflow: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find it on the Tables tab or Bar Tab and tap to open.

docs.title: On Station
docs.text:
1. In the order summary on the left, find the guest with the item → tap three dots for that guest row → tap Move Items
  2. On the left, check the box for the item being moved
  3. In the center, choose the destination — whole table or a different guest

docs.title: On Flex
docs.text:
1. Tap the green pay button to open the order summary
  2. Find the guest with the item → tap three dots for that guest row → tap Move Items
  3. Check the box for the item being moved
  4. Select the destination guest from the choices below

docs.title: Moving Tables
docs.text:
Move an entire order to a new table. Not to be confused with Updating Orders → Combining Tables.

Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from another workflow: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find it on the Tables tab or Bar Tab and tap to open.

docs.title: On Station
docs.text:
1. Tap Move Order in the bottom menu bar
  2. Select An Available Table or the Bar
  3. Tap the destination table number → tap Confirm

The order is moved. Tap the destination table to open the order at its new location.

docs.title: On Flex
docs.text:
1. Tap the green pay button (top left) → tap three dots (bottom right) → tap Move Order
  2. Select An Available Table or the Bar
  3. Tap the destination table → tap Confirm

The order is moved. Tap the destination table to open the order at its new location.

docs.title: Reassign Member
docs.text:
Assign or unassign a member to an order that has already been created.

Prerequisite

An order must be open on screen.

  • Continuing from another workflow: the order is already open — continue below.
  • Returning to an existing order: find it on the Tables tab or Bar Tab and tap to open.

docs.title: Open MemberLink
docs.text:
Navigate to the order payment screen:

  • On Station: from the main order screen, tap the green pay button below the order summary.
  • On Flex: tap the green pay button in the top left → tap Pay.

Once on the payment screen, tap the MemberLink button at the bottom. MemberLink opens and shows any member currently assigned to the order.

docs.title: Assign a member
docs.text:
1. Search for the member's name or number, or scan the membership ID card
  2. Tap the member in the search results → tap Assign

docs.title: Unassign a member
docs.text: Tap the red X on the member chip → tap Unassign.

docs.title: Return to the order
docs.text: Tap Done in the top right to return to the payment screen, then tap the back arrow in the bottom navigation menu to return to the order. The member assignment is preserved.
